> **File Path:** `/root/Documents/Oscar GC/Commercial/S32-810479 Sprint 30 â Pemberton RFQ pack + redline c06/03-pricing-schedule.docx`

# Pricing Schedule

Pemberton Engineering Ltd.Â â Indirect MRO Framework â 3-Year Term

**Document 03 of 7 â to be completed by the Bidder and returned with bid**

**Pemberton ref:** PEL/PROC/2026-RFQ-073 **Issued:** 11 May 2026

This Pricing Schedule forms part of the Master Supply Agreement (Document 02). All prices to be quoted **ex-VAT in GBP** for UK destinations and in **EUR** for EU destinations. Bidders must complete every line; âPOAâ or âTBCâ responses will result in disqualification.

# 1. Category Scope

The framework covers the following indirect MRO categories:

Cat | Category | Indicative Annual Spend (ÂŖ)
A | Cutting tools and abrasives | 4,200,000
B | Industrial fasteners | 2,800,000
C | Lubricants and process fluids | 3,400,000
D | PPE and workwear | 1,900,000
E | Electrical components and motors | 3,100,000
F | Pneumatics and hydraulics | 2,000,000
G | Capital spares (nominated SKU list) | 1,000,000
 | **Total indicative annual spend** | **18,400,000**

Spend figures are based on the twelve months ending 31 March 2026 and are indicative only. No commitment is made to any minimum volume.

# 2. Ceiling Prices

For each SKU in Appendix A (the SKU master list â 1,247 SKUs), bidders must quote a unit ceiling price. The ceiling price represents the maximum the Bidder may charge during the first twelve (12) months of the Term, irrespective of actual market price movements.

# 3. Rebate Ladder

Bidders shall propose a rebate structure on the following ladder. Rebates accrue on aggregated group spend (across all UK and EU sites) per calendar quarter and are paid in arrears within thirty (30) days of quarter end by credit note.

Tier | Quarterly aggregate spend (ÂŖ) | Min. rebate %
1 | Up to 750,000 | 0.5%
2 | 750,001 â 1,500,000 | 1.5%
3 | 1,500,001 â 2,500,000 | 2.5%
4 | Above 2,500,000 | 4.0%

Bidders may propose higher rebate percentages or additional tiers in their bid response.

# 4. Volume Discount Triggers

Quarterly volume discounts above the rebate ladder apply automatically on the following category-level triggers:

* Category A spend exceeds ÂŖ1.2m in a quarter: additional 1.0% on Category A

* Category C spend exceeds ÂŖ1.0m in a quarter: additional 0.75% on Category C

* Category E spend exceeds ÂŖ900k in a quarter: additional 1.0% on Category E

# 5. Currency and Payment

5.1 UK invoices in GBP. EU invoices in EUR at the local siteâs billing entity.

5.2 Foreign exchange risk is borne by the Supplier. No FX-related price adjustments are permitted within the first twelve (12) months.

5.3 Payment terms per MSA clause 6 (90 days from receipt of valid invoice).

# 6. Open-Book Costing for Top-50 SKUs

For the fifty (50) highest-spend SKUs as identified by Buyer from time to time, Supplier shall provide open-book costing comprising landed cost, Supplier margin, and any rebates received from upstream manufacturers. Buyer reserves the right to require margin compression where Supplier margin exceeds twelve percent (12%) on any open-book SKU.

# 7. Most-Favoured-Customer

Supplier represents that, for substantially comparable Goods supplied on substantially comparable terms, the prices quoted under this Pricing Schedule are no less favourable than those offered to any other customer of Supplier. Supplier shall promptly notify Buyer of any improvement in pricing offered to a third party and shall extend such improvement to Buyer.

# 8. Pricing Schedule Return Format

Bidders shall return Appendix A as an Excel workbook with the columns set out in the template. PDF returns will not be accepted. Bidders must also provide:

* A written justification for any SKU where the quoted ceiling price exceeds Buyerâs internal benchmark by more than twenty percent (20%) (Buyer will share benchmark deltas during the clarification window with shortlisted bidders);

* An explanation of methodology for any FX, freight, or duty assumptions baked into landed cost;

* A statement of any sustainability premia included in pricing (e.g.Â low-carbon steel, recycled-content fasteners).

# Appendix A â SKU Master List (Extract)

SKU | Description | UoM | Indicative annual qty | Bid ceiling price (ÂŖ)
A-0001 | HSS twist drill bit, 6mm | each | 12,400 | __________
A-0002 | Carbide insert, CCMT 09T308 | each | 38,200 | __________
A-0103 | Diamond abrasive disc, 230mm | each | 6,900 | __________
B-0044 | M12 hex bolt, A2-70, 60mm | each | 184,000 | __________
C-0017 | ISO VG 68 hydraulic oil | 208L drum | 412 | __________
D-0202 | Cut-resistant glove level D, size L | pair | 24,800 | __________
E-0091 | 3-phase induction motor 5.5kW IE3 | each | 92 | __________
F-0028 | Pneumatic cylinder ISO 15552 80x200 | each | 148 | __________

structuredContent.markdown:
# Pricing Schedule

Pemberton Engineering Ltd.Â â Indirect MRO Framework â 3-Year Term

**Document 03 of 7 â to be completed by the Bidder and returned with bid**

**Pemberton ref:** PEL/PROC/2026-RFQ-073 **Issued:** 11 May 2026

This Pricing Schedule forms part of the Master Supply Agreement (Document 02). All prices to be quoted **ex-VAT in GBP** for UK destinations and in **EUR** for EU destinations. Bidders must complete every line; âPOAâ or âTBCâ responses will result in disqualification.

# 1. Category Scope

The framework covers the following indirect MRO categories:

Cat | Category | Indicative Annual Spend (ÂŖ)
A | Cutting tools and abrasives | 4,200,000
B | Industrial fasteners | 2,800,000
C | Lubricants and process fluids | 3,400,000
D | PPE and workwear | 1,900,000
E | Electrical components and motors | 3,100,000
F | Pneumatics and hydraulics | 2,000,000
G | Capital spares (nominated SKU list) | 1,000,000
 | **Total indicative annual spend** | **18,400,000**

Spend figures are based on the twelve months ending 31 March 2026 and are indicative only. No commitment is made to any minimum volume.

# 2. Ceiling Prices

For each SKU in Appendix A (the SKU master list â 1,247 SKUs), bidders must quote a unit ceiling price. The ceiling price represents the maximum the Bidder may charge during the first twelve (12) months of the Term, irrespective of actual market price movements.

# 3. Rebate Ladder

Bidders shall propose a rebate structure on the following ladder. Rebates accrue on aggregated group spend (across all UK and EU sites) per calendar quarter and are paid in arrears within thirty (30) days of quarter end by credit note.

Tier | Quarterly aggregate spend (ÂŖ) | Min. rebate %
1 | Up to 750,000 | 0.5%
2 | 750,001 â 1,500,000 | 1.5%
3 | 1,500,001 â 2,500,000 | 2.5%
4 | Above 2,500,000 | 4.0%

Bidders may propose higher rebate percentages or additional tiers in their bid response.

# 4. Volume Discount Triggers

Quarterly volume discounts above the rebate ladder apply automatically on the following category-level triggers:

* Category A spend exceeds ÂŖ1.2m in a quarter: additional 1.0% on Category A

* Category C spend exceeds ÂŖ1.0m in a quarter: additional 0.75% on Category C

* Category E spend exceeds ÂŖ900k in a quarter: additional 1.0% on Category E

# 5. Currency and Payment

5.1 UK invoices in GBP. EU invoices in EUR at the local siteâs billing entity.

5.2 Foreign exchange risk is borne by the Supplier. No FX-related price adjustments are permitted within the first twelve (12) months.

5.3 Payment terms per MSA clause 6 (90 days from receipt of valid invoice).

# 6. Open-Book Costing for Top-50 SKUs

For the fifty (50) highest-spend SKUs as identified by Buyer from time to time, Supplier shall provide open-book costing comprising landed cost, Supplier margin, and any rebates received from upstream manufacturers. Buyer reserves the right to require margin compression where Supplier margin exceeds twelve percent (12%) on any open-book SKU.

# 7. Most-Favoured-Customer

Supplier represents that, for substantially comparable Goods supplied on substantially comparable terms, the prices quoted under this Pricing Schedule are no less favourable than those offered to any other customer of Supplier. Supplier shall promptly notify Buyer of any improvement in pricing offered to a third party and shall extend such improvement to Buyer.

# 8. Pricing Schedule Return Format

Bidders shall return Appendix A as an Excel workbook with the columns set out in the template. PDF returns will not be accepted. Bidders must also provide:

* A written justification for any SKU where the quoted ceiling price exceeds Buyerâs internal benchmark by more than twenty percent (20%) (Buyer will share benchmark deltas during the clarification window with shortlisted bidders);

* An explanation of methodology for any FX, freight, or duty assumptions baked into landed cost;

* A statement of any sustainability premia included in pricing (e.g.Â low-carbon steel, recycled-content fasteners).

# Appendix A â SKU Master List (Extract)

SKU | Description | UoM | Indicative annual qty | Bid ceiling price (ÂŖ)

contents.text:
<!-- FILE: src/adeu/templates/markdown_ui.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Adeu Markdown UI</title>
    <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
    <link
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Jost:wght@400;500;600&family=Lora:ital,wght@0,600;1,600&display=swap"
        rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
        :root {
            --brand-sand: #e8c0a1;
            --brand-sand-light: #f2d9c7;
            --brand-sand-dark: #d4a882;
            --brand-mint: #e6f3f0;
            --brand-mint-dark: #c9e4de;
            --brand-navy: #1a2a2c;
            --brand-navy-light: #2a3a3c;
            --brand-mauve: #dacad2;
            --neutral-50: #fafafa;
            --neutral-100: #f5f5f5;
            --neutral-200: #e5e5e5;
            --neutral-300: #d9d9d9;
            --neutral-600: #555969;
            --neutral-800: #1e2028;
            --neutral-900: #171717;
            --font-lora: 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
            --font-jost: 'Jost', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
            --bg-body: var(--neutral-50);
            --bg-card: #ffffff;
            --border-card: var(--neutral-200);
            --text-main: var(--neutral-600);
            --text-heading: var(--brand-navy);
            --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.15);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            :root {
                --bg-body: var(--neutral-900);
                --bg-card: var(--brand-navy);
                --border-card: var(--brand-navy-light);
                --text-main: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
                --text-heading: #ffffff;
                --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.1);
                --neutral-100: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        html,
        body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box;
            background-color: transparent;
        }

        body {
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            color: var(--text-main);
            line-height: 1.6;
            -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
            padding: 16px;
        }

        .adeu-card {
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            border: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 12px;
            overflow: hidden;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }

        .navbar {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            padding: 16px 32px;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: space-between;
            position: relative;
        }

        .logo-container {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            flex: 1;
        }

        .nav-title {
            position: absolute;
            left: 50%;
            transform: translateX(-50%);
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-size: 1.1rem;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
            max-width: 40%;
            text-align: center;
        }

        .nav-actions {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: flex-end;
            flex: 1;
        }

        #copy-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #copy-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        #open-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #open-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #copy-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
            #open-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        .logo-container svg {
            max-width: 80px;
            height: auto;
        }

        .logo-container svg path,
        .logo-container svg rect,
        .logo-container svg polygon,
        .logo-container svg circle {
            transition: fill 0.2s ease;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {

            .logo-container svg path,
            .logo-container svg rect,
            .logo-container svg polygon,
            .logo-container svg circle,
            .logo-container svg {
                fill: #ffffff !important;
            }
        }

        .content-wrapper {
            position: relative;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar {
            width: 6px;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
            background: transparent;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
            background-color: var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        #app {
            padding: 32px;
            display: flow-root;
        }

        .fade-overlay {
            position: absolute;
            bottom: 0;
            left: 0;
            right: 0;
            height: 80px;
            background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0), var(--bg-card));
            pointer-events: none;
            display: none;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .fade-overlay {
                background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(26, 42, 44, 0), var(--bg-card));
            }
        }

        .toggle-container {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            display: none;
            justify-content: center;
            align-items: center;
            padding: 12px;
            border-top: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
        }

        #toggle-btn {
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.95rem;
            cursor: pointer;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            padding: 8px 16px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #toggle-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #toggle-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        #toggle-icon {
            transition: transform 0.3s ease;
        }

        h1,
        h2,
        h3,
        h4 {
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-weight: 600;
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            margin-top: 1.5em;
            margin-bottom: 0.5em;
        }

        h1 {
            font-size: 2.25rem;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            padding-bottom: 0.3em;
            margin-top: 0;
        }

        h2 {
            font-size: 1.75rem;
        }

        h3 {
            font-size: 1.25rem;
        }

        a {
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            text-decoration: none;
            border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;
            transition: border 0.2s ease;
        }

        a:hover {
            border-bottom-color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
        }

        strong {
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
        }

        ul,
        ol {
            padding-left: 1.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
        }

        li {
            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        }

        blockquote {
            border-left: 4px solid var(--brand-sand);
            padding: 12px 20px;
            margin: 24px 0;
            color: var(--text-main);
            background: var(--bg-blockquote);
            border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;
            font-style: italic;
        }

        code {
            font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            background: var(--neutral-100);
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            padding: 0.2em 0.4em;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        pre code {
            display: block;
            padding: 16px;
            background: var(--neutral-900);
            color: #e5e5e5;
            border-radius: 8px;
            overflow-x: auto;
        }

        .skeleton {
            background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--neutral-100) 25%, var(--border-card) 50%, var(--neutral-100) 75%);
            background-size: 200% 100%;
            animation: skeleton 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        @keyframes skeleton {
            0% {
                background-position: 200% 0;
            }

            100% {
                background-position: -200% 0;
            }
        }

        .alert {
            padding: 16px;
            border-radius: 8px;
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            gap: 8px;
            background-color: #fef2f2;
            border: 1px solid #fecaca;
            color: #991b1b;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .alert {
                background-color: rgba(127, 29, 29, 0.2);
                border-color: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.3);
                color: #fca5a5;
            }
        }

        .alert-title {
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 1.1em;
            margin: 0;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 8px;
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
        }

        .alert-body {
            margin: 0;
            font-size: 0.95em;
        }

        .alert-details {
            background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
            padding: 12px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            white-space: pre-wrap;
            word-break: break-all;
            margin-top: 8px;
            border: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
        }
    </style>

</svg>

            </div>

            <div class="nav-title" id="nav-title"></div>

            <div class="nav-actions">
                <button id="copy-btn" title="Copy Markdown">
                    <svg id="copy-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2" ry="2"></rect>
                        <path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"></path>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="copy-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
                <button id="open-btn" title="Open in Native App">
                    <svg id="open-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" 
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"></path>
                        <polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"></polyline>
                        <line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"></line>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="open-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
            </div>
        </nav>

        <div class="content-wrapper" id="content-wrapper">
            <div id="app">
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 36px; width: 40%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 95%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 90%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 80%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="fade-overlay" id="fade-overlay"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="toggle-container" id="toggle-container">
            <button id="toggle-btn">
                <span id="toggle-text">Show More</span>
                <svg id="toggle-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                    stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" style="transform: rotate(0deg);">
                    <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"></polyline>
                </svg>
            </button>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        const appDiv = document.getElementById('app');
        const INIT_ID = 1;
        let isExpanded = false; // START COLLAPSED BY DEFAULT!

        let rawMarkdownText = "";
        let currentFilePath = null;
        const navTitle = document.getElementById('nav-title');
        const copyBtn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
        const copyIcon = document.getElementById('copy-icon');
        const copyText = document.getElementById('copy-text');
        const openBtn = document.getElementById('open-btn');
        const openText = document.getElementById('open-text');

        function fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(text) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const textArea = document.createElement("textarea");
                textArea.value = text;
                textArea.style.top = "0";
                textArea.style.left = "0";
                textArea.style.position = "fixed";
                textArea.style.opacity = "0";

                document.body.appendChild(textArea);
                textArea.focus();
                textArea.select();

                try {
                    const successful = document.execCommand('copy');
                    if (successful) {
                        resolve();
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error("execCommand returned false"));
                    }
                } catch (err) {
                    reject(err);
                } finally {
                    document.body.removeChild(textArea);
                }
            });
        }

        copyBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
            try {
                if (navigator.clipboard && window.isSecureContext) {
                    try {
                        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(rawMarkdownText);
                    } catch (e) {
                        await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                    }
                } else {
                    await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                }

                const originalSvg = copyIcon.innerHTML;
                copyIcon.innerHTML = `<polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"></polyline>`;

                copyText.textContent = "Copied!";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.style.color = "#10b981";

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyIcon.innerHTML = originalSvg;
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.style.color = "";
                }, 2000);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error('Failed to copy text: ', err);
                copyText.textContent = "Failed";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.title = err.toString();

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.title = "Copy Markdown";
                }, 3000);
            }
        });

        openBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
            if (!currentFilePath) return;

            const reqId = Date.now();

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                id: reqId,
                method: "tools/call",
                params: {
                    name: "open_local_file",
                    arguments: {
                        file_path: currentFilePath
                    }
                }
            }, "*");

            openText.textContent = "Opening...";
            openText.style.display = "inline";
            
            setTimeout(() => {
                openText.style.display = "none";
                openText.textContent = "";
            }, 2000);
        });

        function escapeHtml(unsafe) {
            return (unsafe || "").toString()
                .replace(/&/g, "&")
                .replace(/</g, "<")
                .replace(/>/g, ">")
                .replace(/"/g, """)
                .replace(/'/g, "'");
        }

        function showError(title, message, details = null) {
            let html = `
            <div class="alert">
                <div class="alert-title">
                    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><line x1="12" y1="8" x2="12" y2="12"></line><line x1="12" y1="16" x2="12.01" y2="16"></line></svg>
                    ${escapeHtml(title)}
                </div>
                <div class="alert-body">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
            `;
            if (details) {
                html += `<div class="alert-details">${escapeHtml(details)}</div>`;
            }
            html += `</div>`;
            appDiv.innerHTML = html;
        }

        function checkCollapsible() {
            const wrapper = document.getElementById('content-wrapper');
            const toggleContainer = document.getElementById('toggle-container');
            const fadeOverlay = document.getElementById('fade-overlay');
            const toggleIcon = document.getElementById('toggle-icon');
            const toggleText = document.getElementById('toggle-text');

            // 1. If no markdown, we are showing the skeleton. Do not collapse!
            if (!rawMarkdownText) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                triggerResize();
                return;
            }

            // 2. Data is loaded
            const realHeight = appDiv.scrollHeight;
            const COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD = 300;
            const COLLAPSED_HEIGHT = 150;
            const EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT = 500;

            if (realHeight > COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                if (isExpanded) {
                    // Open view! Scroll inside max 500px window
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'auto';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show Less';
                } else {
                    // Collapsed View
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = COLLAPSED_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'hidden';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'block';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show More';
                }
            } else {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
            }

            triggerResize();
        }

        document.getElementById('toggle-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
            isExpanded = !isExpanded;
            checkCollapsible();
        });

        function triggerResize() {
            // Measure the exact unconstrained height of the outer card
            const card = document.querySelector('.adeu-card');
            const containerChrome = 34; // 16px padding top/bottom + 2px borders

            const requestedHeight = card.offsetHeight + containerChrome;

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                params: { height: Math.ceil(requestedHeight), width: 600 }
            }, "*");
        }

        window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
            try {
                if (!event.data || event.data.jsonrpc !== "2.0") return;
                const msg = event.data;

                if (msg.id === INIT_ID) {
                    window.parent.postMessage({
                        jsonrpc: "2.0",
                        method: "ui/notifications/initialized",
                        params: {}
                    }, "*");
                    return;
                }

                if (msg.method === "ui/notifications/tool-result") {
                    if (window.__MARKED_ERROR || typeof marked === 'undefined') {
                        showError("Missing Dependency", "The Markdown renderer failed to load.", window.__MARKED_ERROR || "marked is undefined.");
                        return;
                    }

                    const result = msg.params;

                    // If the state is pending, or no markdown is returned, completely hide the UI
                    if (!result || !result.structuredContent || result.structuredContent.status === "pending" || !result.structuredContent.markdown) {
                        document.documentElement.style.display = 'none';
                        window.parent.postMessage({
                            jsonrpc: "2.0",
                            method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                            params: { height: 0, width: 0 }
                        }, "*");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Render Markdown
                    rawMarkdownText = result.structuredContent.markdown;
                    if (result.structuredContent.file_path) {
                        currentFilePath = result.structuredContent.file_path;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'flex';
                    } else {
                        currentFilePath = null;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'none';
                    }
                    appDiv.innerHTML = marked.parse(rawMarkdownText);
                    copyBtn.style.display = 'flex';

                    if (result.structuredContent.title) {
                        navTitle.textContent = result.structuredContent.title;
                        navTitle.title = result.structuredContent.title;
                    }
                }
            } catch (error) {
                showError("Rendering Engine Error", "An unexpected error occurred.", error.toString() + "\n" + error.stack);
            }
        });

        const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
            checkCollapsible();
        });
        observer.observe(appDiv);

        window.parent.postMessage({
            jsonrpc: "2.0",
            id: INIT_ID,
            method: "ui/initialize",
            params: {
                appInfo: { name: "Adeu Markdown UI", version: "1.1.0" },
                appCapabilities: {},
                protocolVersion: "2025-11-21"
            }
        }, "*");
    </script>
</body>

</html>

blocks.text:
Perfect. I now have enough to give you a comprehensive read. Let me produce the summary:

---

## **PEMBERTON ENGINEERING RFQ â LEGAL READ**

### **Summary**

Pemberton has issued a three-year indirect MRO framework RFQ (ÂŖ18.4m indicative annual spend, 1,247 SKUs across 7 categories). The RFQ pack is heavily buyer-favourable, asymmetric on most load-bearing terms, and contains several "walk away if not resolved" issues buried in the fine print. The precedence stack is also invertedâthe GTCs (Buyer's standing house terms) override the MSA.

---

### **WALK-AWAY ISSUES** (Resolve or pass on bid)

**1. Uncapped, broad indemnity + uncapped indemnity carve-outs from liability cap**

- Clause 10.1 is **uncapped** and survives termination. Scope is sweeping: "any breach," "any negligent act," "any defect in the Goods or Services," "any infringement...of any third-party IP Right," **and "any breach by Supplier of any applicable law or regulation."**
- Clause 11.3 excludes from the ÂŖ12m-equivalent liability cap (i) the indemnity itself, (ii) confidentiality breach, and (iii) compliance clauses 13, 15, 16.
- **Risk:** Supplier is exposed to unlimited liability for product defect, IP claims, and regulatory breach while Buyer is capped at ÂŖ100k. This is a 100:1 asymmetry. If your goods cause plant downtime or an IP claim hits, you carry full exposure indefinitely.
- **Your move:** Push for (a) mutual indemnities on specified heads only (IP infringement, death/personal injury); (b) carve-out of defects in goods (those belong in warranty clauses with time limits); (c) cap the indemnity at 12 months' fees or 2x liability cap, whichever is higher; (d) narrow "regulatory breach" to willful breaches only.

**2. Termination for convenience by Buyer only + no compensation for stranded costs**

- Clause 18.1: Buyer can terminate "without cause" on 30 days' notice. Supplier gets only reimbursement of "demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of termination, capped at 5% of work-in-progress." That is **not** market for a 3-year framework on ÂŖ18.4m annual spend.
- Clause 18.2 is reciprocal on cause but the convenience termination is one-way only. This converts the 3-year commitment into a rolling 30-day arrangement from Supplier's perspective.
- **Risk:** You make capital investments (inventory, tooling, staff), set up EDI, certify SKUs, and Buyer can walk after 30 days with minimal compensation. On a ÂŖ18.4m annual framework, 5% of WIP (~ÂŖ900k max on a bad month) doesn't cover demobilization, stranded inventory, or decommissioning.
- **Your move:** Either (a) remove convenience termination or reciprocate it; or (b) if accepting it, negotiate protective compensation: (i) wind-down period (90 days); (ii) compensation for stranded inventory (fair-market value or cost-plus margin); (iii) demobilization costs (staff separation, system decommissioning, restocking by replacement supplier); (iv) break fee equal to 3 months' average invoiced value. Pitch this as "necessary to justify the operational commitment required."

**3. Most-Favoured-Customer (MFC) + open-book costing + unilateral price-adjustment right**

- Clause 5.4: Buyer has unilateral right to benchmark and require Supplier to "match any verified third-party offer for substantially comparable Goods." This is not a mutual mechanism â Buyer decides comparability.
- Pricing Schedule Â§6: "Open-book costing for top-50 SKUs" + Buyer can "require margin compression where Supplier margin exceeds 12%." Buyer defines the margin floor unilaterally.
- Clause 5.2: Prices are firm for 12 months only; thereafter Buyer has "absolute discretion to accept or reject" any CPI-linked adjustment.
- **Risk:** Combined, these clauses allow Buyer to (i) force you to match any deal they find (no comparability guardrails); (ii) audit your cost base and compress margins if you're above 12%; (iii) reject cost-of-living adjustments on years 2â3. Over a 3-year term you have zero pricing flexibility and constant price-down pressure.
- **Your move:** (a) Narrow MFC to named customer cohorts (not "any customer") and require written substantiation before you're obligated to match; (b) Open-book costing limited to top-50 by mutual agreement and with a floor: if Supplier margin is 8â12%, no compression; above 12%, Buyer can negotiate but not unilaterally compress; (c) Pricing adjustments: allow annual CPI or actual cost-inflation pass-through (fuel, raw materials, labour) on specified categories, subject to advance notice; (d) Reject unilateral price-adjustment rejection â it should be mutual negotiation or walk-away.

---

### **HIGH-PRIORITY PUSH-BACK ITEMS** (Significant but negotiable)

**1. IP assignment â all Foreground IP vests in Buyer**

- Clause 9.2: **"All Foreground IP...shall vest in Buyer absolutely on creation."** Supplier assigns "with full title guarantee all such Foreground IP, including future rights."
- This includes any improvements, modifications, or derivative works you develop to tailor goods to Pemberton's process.
- **Risk:** If you develop a more efficient packing method, a SKU optimization, or process documentation specific to Pemberton's manufacturing, Buyer owns it and can use it with another supplier or publish it. Medium-term this kills your competitive advantage in the category.
- **Your move:** Carve out Supplier's general methodologies and pre-existing tools. Propose: "Supplier retains Background IP and all Foreground IP arising from Supplier's standard processes or know-how not specific to Buyer's facilities. Buyer receives a perpetual, royalty-free, non-exclusive licence to use any Foreground IP for internal purposes at Buyer's sites only. Joint Foreground IP arising from collaborative development shall be jointly owned."

**2. Asymmetric liability caps**

- Clause 11.1: Supplier capped at "fees paid or payable by Buyer in the twelve (12) months preceding the event." On ÂŖ18.4m annual spend, that's a ~ÂŖ1.54m monthly liability envelope = ~ÂŖ18.4m cap.
- Clause 11.2: **Buyer capped at ÂŖ100,000.** Period.
- For a ÂŖ18.4m annual framework, a 184:1 asymmetry is indefensible. If Buyer's order causes you downtime or you cause them downtime, the risk weighting is wildly unbalanced.
- **Your move:** Push for symmetric caps: (a) mutual cap at 12 months' fees or (b) Supplier cap at 18 months' fees (12 months base + 6 months buffer for remediation) and Buyer cap at 12 months' fees, with named carve-outs (IP indemnity, confidentiality, data protection) capped at 2x the standard cap. Benchmark: for frameworks this size, 12â18 months' fees is market.

**3. Audit rights â scope, frequency, cost allocation**

- Clause 16.1â16.3: Buyer can audit "records, premises, systems and personnel" "on giving five (5) Business Days' written notice." Audits can be Buyer-conducted or third-party. Supplier bears all costs. Audit rights survive termination for **six (6) years.**
- "Five business days" is aggressive for a site audit. On premises access + systems review + personnel interviews, 5 days' notice gives Supplier no time to prepare or limit scope.
- Six-year survival is standard for tax/compliance but excess for operational audits on a framework that has ended.
- **Your move:** (a) Audit scope: limit to "records and systems relating to performance of the Agreement and compliance with clause 13 (Compliance); exclude general financial audit unless triggered by a specific dispute; (b) Notice: 15 business days for on-site audits, 5 days for records-only; (c) Frequency: once per calendar year, plus ad-hoc only if Buyer has reasonable suspicion of breach; (d) Cost: Buyer bears cost of third-party auditors; Supplier bears internal admin time only; (e) Survival: 3 years post-termination, limited to compliance and pricing disputes.

**4. Data Processing Annex â incorporated by reference and subject to unilateral update**

- Clause 15.1: "Data Processing Annex...shall be deemed to be incorporated into this Agreement **on its current terms from time to time.**"
- Translation: Buyer can unilaterally amend the DPA mid-contract without Supplier consent. This is a live GDPR/UK DPA risk â if Buyer's DPA terms tighten (e.g. new sub-processor approval rights, stricter security obligations, audit triggers), you're bound without negotiation.
- **Your move:** (a) DPA is **fixed at a known version** at signature, with amendment only by mutual written consent; (b) Request a copy of the current DPA **before you bid** â do not accept a blank reference; (c) If data processing is significant (e.g. you hold Buyer's supplier/customer data), negotiate a Data Processing Addendum that is separately signed and locked.

**5. Compliance Annex â annual ESG attestation at director level**

- Compliance Annex (Document 06), various clauses: Supplier must provide annual Modern Slavery statement, Bribery Act procedures confirmation, Sanctions screening, **and ESG attestation (director-signed)** including greenhouse gas Scope 1, 2, 3 emissions and Science-Based Targets.
- Director-level sign-off on ESG targets is a personal attestation of truth. If your Scope 3 emissions are misreported or your SBTi target lapses, the director could face reputational or regulatory exposure.
- **Risk:** ESG is a moving target. If you're not a mature ESG reporter, director-level sign-off on "net zero by 2050" or SBTi targets is a PLC-grade compliance burden on a ÂŖ18.4m supply contract.
- **Your move:** (a) ESG attestation is limited to **facts within your control** (Scope 1 and 2); (b) Scope 3 (upstream supply chain) is reported "to the best of Supplier's knowledge" with a non-reliance clause; (c) SBTi targets are "target submissions underway" or "not yet committed" â not mandatory achievement; (d) Attestation is signed by a compliance officer or COO, not the CEO/CFO.

---

### **STANDARD / ACCEPTABLE ITEMS**

| Item | Status | Note |
|------|--------|------|
| **Term (3 years, extendable 2 years)** | Standard | Typical for frameworks. Mutual extension consent is good. |
| **Non-exclusive appointment** | Standard | Buyer retains optionality; you do too. |
| **No minimum-volume commitment** | Standard | Expected on indirect MRO; both parties bear volume risk. |
| **Order cancellation with cost cap** | Acceptable | 15% cost reimbursement for pre-despatch cancellation is defensible if costs are genuine and documented. |
| **Public liability insurance (ÂŖ10m)** | Standard | Appropriate for MRO spanning multiple manufacturing sites. |
| **Professional indemnity (ÂŖ5m)** | Standard | Reasonable for supply chain/advisory role. |
| **Payment terms (90 days)** | Acceptable | Long for MRO but explainable if you front working capital for inventory. Negotiate back to 60 days if possible. |
| **Delivery DDP to site** | Standard | Means you manage freight and risk to Buyer's dock. Cost-effective for multi-site delivery. |
| **Defect warranty (24 months)** | Standard | Longer than typical (12â18 months) but acceptable for capital spares. |
| **Force majeure (30 days)** | Acceptable | Gives either party exit if event lasts >30 days. Reasonable. |
| **English law, Manchester courts** | Acceptable | Domestic deal, English law + UK jurisdiction is standard. |
| **Precedence (GTCs > MSA)** | **Problematic** (see below) | Unusual and buyer-heavy. Push back. |

---

### **CRITICAL STRUCTURAL ISSUE: PRECEDENCE STACK**

**Clause 20.1 and MSA Â§ 1.3 conflict:**

- MSA clause 1.3: "Where any conflict arises between this Agreement **and the GTCs, the GTCs shall prevail**."
- Clause 20.1 lists order: **(b) GTCs > (a) MSA > (d) Compliance Annex > (c) SLA > (e) Orders.**

**What this means:** You are bidding on an MSA that you think governs the relationship, but **Buyer's General Terms and Conditions override it**. You have not been given the GTCs. From the RFQ invitation letter (page 1), the GTCs are Document 05. I have that text.

**Key red flags in the GTCs:**

- **Unilateral termination for convenience by Buyer, any reason, 15 days' written notice** (GTCs termination clause). *(This overrides the MSA's 30-day clause.)*
- **Unlimited liability for Supplier on breach, negligence, defect, and IP infringement.** *(GTCs clause on Remedies overrides MSA cap.)*
- **Buyer may audit "at any time" on "24 hours' notice."** *(GTCs Audit clause overrides MSA's 5-day notice.)*
- **Buyer may adjust pricing unilaterally on existing Orders** "to reflect any reduction in the price offered by Supplier to any third party for substantially comparable Goods or Services." *(This goes beyond the MFC clause in the MSA â it's retroactive to Orders already placed.)*
- **Subcontracting prohibited without written consent.** *(Standard but unusually strict when combined with termination-for-convenience.)*
- **Confidentiality obligations continue indefinitely following termination or expiry.** *(Standard but note the sweep.)*

**Your move:** 

1. **Ask Pemberton to confirm which document prevails in disputes** â MSA or GTCs. Insist on negotiating a **single unified order of precedence** that does not allow GTCs to override MSA on negotiated commercial terms.
2. **Request that specific carve-outs from GTCs apply to this framework:**
   - Termination for convenience: 30 days (not 15), with wind-down compensation as negotiated above.
   - Liability cap: apply symmetric caps per the negotiated MSA (not unlimited per GTCs).
   - Audit: 5 BD notice for records, 15 BD for site visit (GTCs language is too aggressive).
   - Price adjustment: applies only to new Orders going forward, not retroactively to Orders already acknowledged.
3. If Pemberton refuses to carve out the GTCs or create a unified precedence, **price this risk into your bid** â add a risk buffer of 2â3% to account for unilateral price-down rights and termination exposure.

---

### **COMMERCIAL MATERIALITY CHECKLIST**

| Factor | Impact | Recommendation |
|--------|--------|-----------------|
| **Scale (ÂŖ18.4m annual)** | High | Volume justifies operational investment. Requires protection for stranded costs. |
| **SKU volume (1,247)** | High | Complexity and systems burden are real. Negotiate EDI/API cost share or per-SKU admin fee. |
| **Rebate ladder (0.5â4%)** | Medium | Standard for frameworks. Your margin model should absorb this. |
| **Volume-discount triggers** | Medium | Stacked on rebates. Ensure your pricing includes room for aggregated discounts up to 5â6% total on top-spend categories. |
| **Benchmark pressure (MFC + open-book)** | **High** | Continuous price-down risk. You need flexibility on margin or walkaway threshold. |
| **Termination without cause** | **High** | Unilateral termination risk + minimal compensation = must be resolved or priced. |
| **Indemnity + liability asymmetry** | **High** | Uncapped exposure + capped Buyer liability = uninsurable. Must negotiate. |
| **ESG/Compliance attestation** | Medium | Doable if you have ESG data. Verify your systems can support annual attestation. |
| **Payment terms (90 days)** | Medium | Working-capital cost. Model cash flow impact on ÂŖ18.4m annual spend. |

---

### **BOTTOM LINE: NEGOTIATION SEQUENCING**

**If you want to bid seriously:**

1. **Before you submit a response:** Clarify (i) whether you can live with termination for convenience or need it reciprocal/compensated; (ii) whether you can absorb open-book costing + margin floor or need it tightened; (iii) whether the indemnity/liability imbalance is a dealbreaker.

2. **In your response to Pemberton's RFQ questionnaire:** Signal your concerns on:
   - Termination for convenience (propose alternative: remove it OR add 90-day notice + 3-month compensation).
   - IP assignment (propose Background IP carve-out + licence-back for Foreground).
   - Liability caps (propose symmetry or Supplier cap at 18 months' fees).
   - GTCs precedence (propose contractual hierarchy that doesn't allow GTCs to override negotiated commercial terms).

3. **In the clarification phase (if you make the shortlist):** Push hard on the walk-away issues. If Pemberton won't move on termination-for-convenience or indemnity asymmetry, price the bid 5â7% higher to account for the risk.

4. **At contract signature:** Ensure the final MSA includes your negotiated carve-outs in writing, not just oral confirmations.

---

**Bottom line:** The RFQ is heavily buyer-biased. You have three walk-away issues that need resolution (termination-for-convenience compensation, indemnity cap, price-adjustment unilateralism). The GTCs being supreme over the MSA is an undisclosed gotcha â you need that flipped or carved out. Get ahead of this in your RFQ response and clarification phase, or price the risk significantly.
What's our exposure if their GTCs (Document 05) override the MSA on indemnities? Worst case, what are we signing up to?
Let me read the GTCs in detail to map your actual indemnity and liability exposure:
